steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure on each side of the blank
steps.content: The phrase before the blank—”By supplying oil, grapes, and grain to societies that lacked these valuable items”—is an introductory prepositional phrase. The phrase after—”the Roman Empire grew not only in wealth but also in power and influence”—is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for introductory phrases
steps.content:
An introductory phrase must be followed by a comma before the main clause. Choice B correctly places the comma after “items”. Options A and D use wrong punctuation for clause boundaries (semicolon and period are for joining or ending clauses), and option C omits the required comma.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
